---
output:
  word_document:
    reference_docx: templates/markdown_word_template_1.docx
    fig_height: 3.5
    fig_width: 8
bibliography: chapter.bib
csl: transactions-of-the-american-fisheries-society.csl
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warnings = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(flextable)
library(knitr)
```

## Creel Surveys

Kevin L. Pope, U.S. Geological Survey---Nebraska Cooperative Fish and Wildlife Research Unit, and School of Natural Resources, University of Nebraska, Lincoln, Nebraska, USA

Christopher J. Chizinski, School of Natural Resources, University of Nebraska, Lincoln, Nebraska, USA

Abigail J. Lynch, U.S. Geological Survey, National Climate Adaptation Science Center, Reston, Virginia, USA

John R. Post, Department of Biological Sciences, University of Calgary, Calgary, Alberta, Canada

This draft manuscript is distributed solely for purposes of scientific peer review. Its content is deliberative and predecisional, so it must not be disclosed or released by reviewers. Because the manuscript has not yet been approved for publication by the U.S. Geological Survey (USGS), it does not represent any official USGS finding or policy.

### List of Boxes

**Box 18.1** Generating a randomized creel schedule

**Box 18.2** Difficulty of converting "experimental units" of interest

**Box 18.3** Generating estimates of effort

**Box 18.4** Comparison of total effort using Mean Daily estimator and Strata estimator

**Box 18.5** Estimates of catch

**Box 18.6** Using bipartite networks to explore released fish

\newpage

### Box 18.1 Generating a randomized creel schedule

Let us consider a single launch site with a parking area for boaters to access a river. We wish to estimate angling pressure on this river from this single launch site. We know from prior investigation that all boaters using this launch do so for the purpose of fishing. We also know that use of this launch site is often an order of magnitude greater on weekend days than week days, and that there is seasonality in this fishery. Thus, we decide to conduct an assessment of fishing pressure by instantaneous counts of boat-trailers in the parking lot during April-October with sampling effort stratified by month and day type (weekend, week).

*Load libraries: tidyverse and lubridate.*

```{r loadpackages1, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
```

*Generating a sequence of dates that represent the creel sampling period*

```{r createCalendar}
calendar <- tibble(date = seq(ymd("2019-04-01"), ymd("2019-10-31"), by = "1 day"))

calendar %>%
  mutate(
    month = month(date, label = TRUE),
    dayWeek = wday(date, label = TRUE),
    strata = ifelse(dayWeek %in% c("Sat", "Sun"), "weekendday", "weekday"),
    group = 1
  ) %>%
  expand_grid(shift = c("morning", "afternoon")) -> calendarFull
```

```{r echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
kable(head(calendar), caption = "The first six rows of the dates for a creel schedule.", col.names = "Date", booktabs = TRUE)
```

*Define how you treat holidays or other high-use days.*

There is the potential that some high-use periods (labor day weekend) that can span across a month. To address this, and keep the estimates within those periods and not split across months, we use **group**, which is just a way of identifying those different high-use periods across creel sampling year. For the below example, we are going to treat holidays in with the "weekend day" strata.

```{r createCalendar2}
# Memorial day weekend
calendarFull$strata[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-05-25"),
  ymd("2019-05-26"),
  ymd("2019-05-27")
)] <- "weekendday"

calendarFull$group[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-05-25"),
  ymd("2019-05-26"),
  ymd("2019-05-27")
)] <- 1

# July 4th
calendarFull$strata[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-07-04"),
  ymd("2019-07-05"),
  ymd("2019-07-06"),
  ymd("2019-07-06")
)] <- "weekendday"

calendarFull$group[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-07-04"),
  ymd("2019-07-05"),
  ymd("2019-07-06"),
  ymd("2019-07-06")
)] <- 2
```

```{r echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
kable(calendarFull %>% filter(date %in% c(ymd("2019-05-23"), ymd("2019-05-24"), ymd("2019-05-25"), ymd("2019-05-26"), ymd("2019-05-27"), ymd("2019-05-28"), ymd("2019-05-29"))), caption = "The first six rows of a creel schedule showing how you might treat holidays or other special days.", col.names = c("Date", "Month", "Day of the week", "Strata", "Group", "Shift"), booktabs = TRUE)
```


*Randomly sampling dates from the full calendar using the `purrr` package (part of tidyverse).* For the below example, we are going to sample 6 weekend days and 10 week days in a month. Further, we are going to equally split the number of morning shifts and afternoon shifts for each of the strata. Setting the seed, ensures that we will get the same random selection of days.

```{r}
set.seed(1234)
numWeekends <- 6
numWeekdays <- 10

# sample morning shift
calendarFull %>%
  filter(shift == "morning") %>%
  group_by(month, strata, group, shift) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(daystoSample = ifelse(strata == "weekendday", numWeekends / 2, numWeekdays / 2)) %>% # dividing by two, for mornign shift
  mutate(sampledDays = map2(data, daystoSample, sample_n)) %>%
  select(month, shift, sampledDays, group, strata) %>%
  unnest(sampledDays) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(date, month, dayWeek, shift, group, strata) %>%
  mutate(month = as.character(month)) -> CreelSchedule_morning


# sample afternoon shifts
calendarFull %>%
  filter(
    shift == "afternoon",
    !date %in% CreelSchedule_morning$date
  ) %>%
  group_by(month, strata, group, shift) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(daystoSample = ifelse(strata == "weekendday", numWeekends / 2, numWeekdays / 2)) %>% # dividing by two, for afternoon shift
  mutate(sampledDays = map2(data, daystoSample, sample_n)) %>%
  select(month, shift, sampledDays, group, strata) %>%
  unnest(sampledDays) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(date, month, dayWeek, shift, group, strata) %>%
  mutate(month = as.character(month)) -> CreelSchedule_afternoon

# combine the two into a single data frame
rbind(CreelSchedule_morning, CreelSchedule_afternoon) %>%
  arrange(date) -> creelSchedule
```

```{r echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
kable(head(creelSchedule), caption = "The first six rows of a *randomized* creel schedule showing strata and creel shift.", col.names = c("Date", "Month", "Day of the week", "Strata", "Group", "Shift"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

*Create a function to sample count times during the creel shifts* Generating count times during a creel shift has several complications. One, is at what time interval do you want to sample from (e.g., every minute, every 15 minutes)? Two, how much of a time interval do you want between counts? When having more than one count during a creel shift, requires that you remove some potential count times from the pool dependent on the time that is first selected. The easiest way to do this is using a `while` loop inside a function to sample your count times.

```{r generateCounttimes}
count_times <- function(nCounts = 3, timeInterval = 15, timeBtwn = 60, startTime, endTime) {
  seq(ymd_hm(paste("2019-01-01", startTime)),
    ymd_hm(paste("2019-01-01", endTime)),
    by = paste(timeInterval, "min")
  ) -> timePeriod

  countTimes <- tibble(time = mdy_hms(rep(NA, nCounts)))
  c <- 1
  while (c <= nCounts) {
    countTimes$time[c] <- sample(timePeriod, 1)
    ind <- which(abs(interval(timePeriod, countTimes$time[c]) / minutes(1)) >= timeBtwn)
    timePeriod <- timePeriod[ind]
    c <- c + 1
  }
  out <- paste(format(sort(countTimes$time), "%T"), collapse = ", ")
  return(out)
}

set.seed(12345)
count_times(startTime = "06:00", endTime = "13:30") # example of using the function
```

*Shift start and end times for each month* The below creel shift start and end times are based on mean sunrise and sunset times in Kearney, Nebraska.

```{r creelstartstop}
rbind(
  tibble(
    month = c("Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"),
    shift = "morning",
    startTime = c("08:00", "08:00", "07:30", "07:30", "07:00", "06:30", "06:00", "06:00", "06:30", "07:00", "07:30", "08:00"),
    endTime = "13:30"
  ),
  tibble(
    month = c("Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"),
    shift = "afternoon",
    startTime = "13:30",
    endTime = c("17:30", "18:00", "19:30", "20:00", "20:30", "21:00", "21:00", "20:30", "19:30", "18:30", "17:30", "17:30")
  )
) -> sunRisesunSet
```

*Join start and stop times with the creel schedule and map the count\_times function for each creel shift*

```{r creelstartstop2}
creelSchedule %>%
  left_join(sunRisesunSet, by = c("month", "shift")) %>%
  mutate(countTimes = map2_chr(startTime, endTime, ~ count_times(nCounts = 3, startTime = .x, endTime = .y))) -> finalCreelSchedule
```

*View the first ten lines of the schedule*

```{r viewSchedule, echo = FALSE}
kable(head(finalCreelSchedule, n = 10), caption = "The first 10 rows of a *randomized* creel schedule showing strata and creel shift.", col.names = c("Date", "Month", "Day of the week", "Shift", "Group", "Strata", "Shift start", "Shift end", "Count times"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

*Using the "high-use" strata*

First we redefine the high use strata. Below we adapt the above code to a "high use" strata and sample those separately from the weekend and weekday days.

```{r highusedays}
# Memorial day weekend
calendarFull$strata[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-05-25"),
  ymd("2019-05-26"),
  ymd("2019-05-27")
)] <- "highuse"

calendarFull$group[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-05-25"),
  ymd("2019-05-26"),
  ymd("2019-05-27")
)] <- 1

# July 4th
calendarFull$strata[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-07-04"),
  ymd("2019-07-05"),
  ymd("2019-07-06"),
  ymd("2019-07-06")
)] <- "highuse"

calendarFull$group[calendarFull$date %in% c(
  ymd("2019-07-04"),
  ymd("2019-07-05"),
  ymd("2019-07-06"),
  ymd("2019-07-06")
)] <- 2
```

*Randomly sampling dates from the full calendar using the `purrr` package for weekend, weekday, and high-use days.*

```{r randomhigh use}
numWeekends <- 6
numWeekdays <- 8
highusedays <- 2

# weekend morning shifts that are not high use
calendarFull %>%
  filter(
    strata != "highuse",
    shift == "morning"
  ) %>%
  group_by(month, strata, group, shift) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(daystoSample = ifelse(strata == "weekendday", numWeekends / 2, numWeekdays / 2)) %>%
  mutate(sampledDays = map2(data, daystoSample, sample_n)) %>%
  select(month, shift, sampledDays, group, strata) %>%
  unnest(sampledDays) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(date, month, dayWeek, shift, group, strata) %>%
  mutate(month = as.character(month)) -> CreelSchedule_morning


# weekend afternoon shifts that are not high use
calendarFull %>%
  filter(
    strata != "highuse",
    shift == "afternoon",
    !date %in% CreelSchedule_morning$date
  ) %>%
  group_by(month, strata, group, shift) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(daystoSample = ifelse(strata == "weekendday", numWeekends / 2, numWeekdays / 2)) %>%
  mutate(sampledDays = map2(data, daystoSample, sample_n)) %>%
  select(month, shift, sampledDays, group, strata) %>%
  unnest(sampledDays) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(date, month, dayWeek, shift, group, strata) %>%
  mutate(month = as.character(month)) -> CreelSchedule_afternoon

# high use morning shifts
calendarFull %>%
  filter(
    strata == "highuse",
    shift == "morning"
  ) %>%
  group_by(month, strata, group, shift) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(daystoSample = highusedays / 2) %>%
  mutate(sampledDays = map2(data, daystoSample, sample_n)) %>%
  select(month, shift, sampledDays, group, strata) %>%
  unnest(sampledDays) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(date, month, dayWeek, shift, group, strata) %>%
  mutate(month = as.character(month)) -> highuse_morning

# high use afternoon shifts
calendarFull %>%
  filter(
    strata == "highuse",
    shift == "afternoon"
  ) %>%
  group_by(month, strata, group, shift) %>%
  nest() %>%
  mutate(daystoSample = highusedays / 2) %>%
  mutate(sampledDays = map2(data, daystoSample, sample_n)) %>%
  select(month, shift, sampledDays, group, strata) %>%
  unnest(sampledDays) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(date, month, dayWeek, shift, group, strata) %>%
  mutate(month = as.character(month)) -> highuse_afternoon

rbind(CreelSchedule_morning, CreelSchedule_afternoon, highuse_morning, highuse_afternoon) %>%
  arrange(date) -> creelSchedule
```

*Join start and stop times with the creel schedule and map the count\_times function for each creel shift*

```{r creelstartstop3}
creelSchedule %>%
  left_join(sunRisesunSet, by = c("month", "shift")) %>%
  mutate(countTimes = map2_chr(startTime, endTime, ~ count_times(nCounts = 3, startTime = .x, endTime = .y))) -> finalCreelScheduleHU
```

*View the first ten lines of the schedule with the high-use strata*

```{r viewSchedule2, echo = FALSE}
kable(head(finalCreelScheduleHU, n = 10), caption = "The first 10 rows of a *randomized* creel schedule with a 'high use' strata.", col.names = c("Date", "Month", "Day of the week", "Shift", "Group", "Strata", "Shift start", "Shift end", "Count times"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

\newpage

### Box 18.2. Difficulty of converting "experimental units" of interest.

Let us consider a hypothetical day for a small impoundment with four fishing parties that fished 4, 4, 4, and 12 hours and comprised 3, 2, 2, and 1 anglers, respectively. If our "experimental unit" (i.e., level at which we are collected interview data) of interest is a party, then fishing effort for this hypothetical day would be 24 party-hours (undefined variance) with an average fishing time per party of 6 hours (variance = 16) and an average party size of 2 anglers (variance = 0.7). Extrapolation of party information would provide an estimate of 48 hours of fishing effort (4 parties Ă— 6 hours/party Ă— 2 anglers/party) for this hypothetical day, but you note that the units in this extrapolation do not carry through (i.e., hours Ă— angler/party does not equal hours)---an important indication that the extrapolation, or conversion in this case, does not jive with the actual effort of 40 hours of fishing (4 hours Ă— 3 anglers + 4 hours Ă— 2 anglers + 4 hours Ă— 2 anglers + 12 hours Ă— 1 angler) for this hypothetical day. If our "experimental unit" of interest is an individual angler, then fishing effort for this hypothetical day would be 40 hours (undefined variance) with an average fishing time of 5 hours (variance = 8) for each of the 8 anglers. The conversion to parties is also difficult; that is, we would estimate the average time spent fishing for each party to be 5 hours compared to the actual average of 6 hours per party. Thus, it is imperative that the level at which data are collected is defined *a priori* and that this level is maintained throughout all assessments.

\newpage

### Box 18.3 Generating estimates of effort

*Load library: tidyverse*
```{r loadpackages, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```


*Instantaneous Counts*

To calculate the effort from an instantaneous count is relatively simple.  If we were to denote the number of anglers counted as *C* and the time interval under consideration as T then the estimated effort is given by:

$$ \hat{f_t} = C \times T$$

For example, suppose a creel clerk was to work a morning creel shift from 06:00 to 14:00 with an instantaneous count occurring at 10:15.  During the count, she counted 20 anglers fishing from boats and 18 fishing from the bank.  The total number of anglers, C, was 38.  The creel shift, T, was 8 hours (14:00 â€“ 06:00).

```{r}
numAnglersCounted <- 38
shiftTime <- 8

shiftPressure <- numAnglersCounted * shiftTime
```

The estimated angler effort for that shift is `r shiftPressure` angler hours.

If multiple counts are conducted, we substitute the mean count, $\bar{C}$  into the equation above.

$$ \hat{f_t} = \frac{\sum(C_i)}{m} \times T$$

where C is the count for each creel count, i, and m is the total number of counts conducted.

If the creel clerk had two counts during the shift rather than the one in the previous example, and these counts were C1 = 0 anglers (during storm) and C2 = 65,

```{r}
numAnglersCounted <- c(0, 65)
shiftTime <- 8

shiftPressure2 <- mean(numAnglersCounted) * shiftTime
```

The estimated effort would be `r shiftPressure2` angler hours.

*Extrapolating from shift to day*
Suppose that the creel shift the clerk was working represented half of the day.
```{r}
dayPressure <- (mean(numAnglersCounted) * shiftTime) / 0.5
```
The estimated effort for that day would be `r dayPressure` angler hours.

*Progressive Counts*

The calculation of effort from a progressive count is also relatively simple.  If we were to denote the number of anglers counted as C and the time interval to complete the circuit around the waterbody, $\tau$, then the estimated effort ($f_p$) is given by:
$$ \hat{f_p} = C \times \tau$$

For example, suppose a creel clerk is working at Lake McConaughy, a 14,000-ha reservoir in western Nebraska.  To travel around the reservoir (i.e., complete the circuit) takes 2 hours.  During the count, he counted 21 anglers fishing from the bank and 213 anglers fishing from boats.  The total number of anglers, C, is 234 anglers.

```{r}
numAnglersCounted <- c(21, 213)
circuitTime <- 2

circuitEffort <- sum(numAnglersCounted) * circuitTime
```

The estimated angler effort for that circuit is `r circuitEffort` 468 angler hours.

Often, we would like to have an estimate for the creel shift, which is representative of a stratum (e.g., morning or afternoon period) used in the creel design. Suppose that the creel shift at Lake McConaughy is 8-hours long (T = 14:00 â€“ 06:00 = 8 hours) and the 2-hour circuit was randomly chosen during that period. During this creel shift there is the potential to complete four circuits ($\kappa$; 8-hour workday / 2-hour circuit).  The effort for the total creel shift can then be calculated as:
$$ \hat{f_p} = C \times \tau \times \kappa$$

```{r}
numAnglersCounted <- c(21, 213)
circuitTime <- 2
numCircuits <- 4

circuitEffort <- sum(numAnglersCounted) * circuitTime * numCircuits
```

The daily effort following this example would be `r circuitEffort` angler hours.

*Bus Route Counts*
Estimating effort is a bit more complicated than the *instantaneous* or *progressive* counts.  For bus routes, we need information on the amount of time each vehicle or trailer is at each boat ramp, the total time (waiting and travel) to complete a circuit.

$$
  \widehat{E} = T\sum\limits_{i=1}^n{\frac{1}{w_{i}}}\sum\limits_{j=1}^m{\frac{e_{ij}}{\pi_{j}}}
$$

where

* *E* = estimated total party-hours of effort,
* *T* = total time to complete a full circuit of the route, including travelling and waiting,
* *w~i~* = waiting time at the *i^th^* site (where *i* = 1, ..., *n* sites), and
* *e~ij~* = total time that the *j^th^* car (or trailer) is parked at the *i^th^* site while the agent is at that site (where *j* = 1, ..., *n* sites).


As an example, suppose we have a survey at Branched Oak Lake (728 ha) in eastern Nebraska, where a creel shift starts at 12:30 and goes until 22:00 (7-hour and 45-minute shift) (Table 18.3.1).  There are 7 access points with travel times to each point ranging from 15 to 30 minutes.  Most of the time a clerk will spend 60 minutes at each access point, except for site 7, which is 75 minutes.

```{r tab1831, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
schedule <- readr::read_csv("data/busroutescheduleExample.csv")
ft <- autofit(flextable(schedule))
ft <- set_caption(ft, "A bus-route schedule used to illustrate the time interval method of estimating effort and catch.  The bus-route schedule covers 7 sites that begins at 12:30.")
ft
```

When completing a bus route effort, each car (or trailer) that is observed at each access point is given a number.  If a car is already at a site when the clerk gets there, it is assigned an arrival time of the time when the clerk reaches the site. Otherwise, the arrival time or departure time of the vehicle is recorded (Table 18.3.2).  Now suppose, during the shift the clerk records five cars at site one (two cars already there, and two that were still there when the clerk departed), two cars at site two (one there at arrival and one at departure), no cars at site three and six, four cars at site four (all there still at departure), three cars at site five (all there at arrival and departure), and six cars at site seven (three at arrival and five at departure).  The basic process is as follows:  1) calculate the total time spent during a creel shift (i.e., sum the wait time and travel time), 2) calculate the time each vehicle spent at each site (i.e., Departure time â€“ Arrival time), 3) sum the times for each site and divide by the probability of sampling day or night, 4) multiply this total by the inverse of wait time at each site and take the sum across all sites, and 5) multiple the total across all sites and multiply by the total minutes of the creel shift (step 1). The result is 16,606 party hours and, if it is assumed that there was an average of 2 anglers per party, then 33,212 angler hours.

```{r tab1832, echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
bsCounts <- readr::read_csv("data/busroutecountsExample.csv")
ft <- autofit(flextable(bsCounts))
ft <- set_caption(ft, "An example of bus-route data collected at a lake with seven sites during and afternoon creel survey. `NA` indicates missing data")
ft
```

*Load library: tidyverse.*
```{r loadpackages3, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
```

*Load data, and check data.*
```{r message = FALSE}
schedule <- read_csv("data/busroutescheduleExample.csv")
head(schedule)

bsCounts <- read_csv("data/busroutecountsExample.csv")
head(bsCounts)
```


```{r echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
kable(head(schedule), caption = "The first six rows of a bus-route creel schedule.", col.names = c("Site", "Scheduled time", "Wait time", "Travel time"), booktabs = TRUE)
```


```{r echo = FALSE, message = FALSE}
kable(head(bsCounts), caption = "The first six rows of the counts from bus-route creel.", col.names = c("Date", "Site", "Boat trailers", "Arrival time", "Departure time"), booktabs = TRUE)
```



*Calculate effort from bus route date*
```{r}
ttlTime <- sum(schedule$WaitTime) + sum(schedule$TravelTime) # calculate the total time of the shift (wait time and travel time)

bsCounts %>%
  mutate(
    timeDiff = departureTime - arrivalTime, # find difference in time
    timeDiff = (as.numeric(timeDiff) / 60) / 0.5
  ) %>% # convert seconds to minutes and divide by the probability of sampling an evening shift
  group_by(Date, Site) %>% # group by date and site
  summarise(ttlTime = sum(timeDiff, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% # calculate total time of all vehicles at each the site
  left_join(schedule, by = "Site") %>% # join the vehicle information with the schedule
  mutate(timeWait = ttlTime * (1 / WaitTime)) %>% # multiply the total time by the inverse of the wait time
  group_by(Date) %>% # group by day
  summarise(ttl = sum(timeWait)) -> count # then take the sum by the date

count$Ehat <- count$ttl * ttlTime
count$Ehat * 2.0
```

\newpage

### Box 18.4.  Comparison of total effort using Mean Daily estimator and Strata estimator.

*Load libraries: tidyverse and lubridate.*
```{r loadpackages4, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
```

*Load count and interview data, check data structure, and limit the species in the interviews to a few species.*
```{r loaddata4, message=FALSE}
juneCount <- read_csv("data/juneCount.csv")

juneInterviews <- read_csv("data/juneInterviews.csv") %>%
  filter(!speciesCaught %in% c("Common Carp", "Crappie", "Muskellunge", "Northern Pike", "Striped Bass Hybrid (wiper)", "Yellow Perch ")) # remove these species
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
# kable(head(juneCount), caption = "The first 6 rows of instantaneous counts from a creel survey.", col.names = c("Date","Period", "Section","Count time", "Bank anglers","Boat anglers"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
# kable(head(juneInterviews), caption = "The first 6 rows of interviews from a creel survey.", col.names = c("Date","Period", "Section", "Number of anglers","Angler type", "Angler method","Time fished (hours)", "Time fished (mins)", "Trip type","Number of fish", "Type of catch", "Species", "Species sought", "Party ID", "Month", "Day of week", "Strata"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

*Mean daily estimator*

The mean-daily estimator takes creel information and digests the effort and associated variance to a single day and then extrapolates that out to the total number of the days in the month.  A step by stamp example for both effort and catch can be found in @malvestuto1978evaluation.  The approach is similar for both estimates.  For the purpose of this box and to simplify the calculations, we will focus this box on estimating effort.  In this example, we are going use sample data from a single month from Calamus reservoir (~2000 ha) in central Nebraska.

*Calculate the mean angler count for boat and bank anglers per shift*
```{r}
juneCount %>%
  group_by(date, period, section) %>%
  summarise(
    meanBank = mean(bankAnglers),
    meanBoat = mean(boatAnglers)
  ) -> dailyMeanCount

head(dailyMeanCount)
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(head(dailyMeanCount), caption = "Mean bank and boat anglers per creel day.", col.names = c("Date", "Period", "Section", "Mean bank anglers", "Mean boat anglers"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

*Convert the mean angler counts per shift to angler effort per day*
```{r}
dailyMeanCount %>%
  mutate(
    bankHours = meanBank * 8, # creel shifts are 8 hrs long
    boatHours = meanBoat * 8,
    bankDayHours = bankHours / 0.5, # morning and afternoon shifts represent half of the day
    boatDayHours = boatHours / 0.5
  ) -> dailyEffort

head(dailyEffort)
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(head(dailyEffort), caption = "Mean effort per creel day.", col.names = c("Date", "Period", "Section", "Mean bank anglers", "Mean boat anglers", "Bank effort per shift", "Boat effort per shift", "Bank effort per day", "Boat effort per day"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

*Calculate the mean daily effort within each strata (weekdays, weekend days)*
```{r}
dailyEffort %>%
  ungroup() %>% # remove the previous grouping
  pivot_longer(bankDayHours:boatDayHours, names_to = "anglerType", values_to = "dailyEffort") %>% # move from wide format to long format
  mutate(
    anglerType = case_when(
      anglerType == "bankDayHours" ~ "Bank", # simplify angler type
      anglerType == "boatDayHours" ~ "Boat"
    ),
    date = ymd(date), # use lubridate to convert date to a date object
    month = month(date, label = TRUE), # get the month for each date
    strata = ifelse(wday(date, label = TRUE) %in% c("Sat", "Sun"), "weekendday", "weekday")
  ) %>% # identify whether each day is a weekend day or week day
  group_by(month, anglerType, strata) %>%
  summarise(
    meanDailyEffort = mean(dailyEffort), # calculate mean daily effort
    varDailyEffort = var(dailyEffort), # calculate variance of effort
    nDaysSampled = n()
  ) -> meanDailyEffort # calculate the actual number of days sampled
```

*Calculate a weighted daily estimate*
```{r warning = FALSE}
# create a data frame that describes the number of weekdays, weekend days, and total days per month
tibble(
  month = "Jun",
  strata = c("weekday", "weekendday"),
  nDays = c(8, 22),
  ttlDays = 30
) -> daysPerMonth

left_join(meanDailyEffort, daysPerMonth, by = c("month", "strata")) %>% # join the days per month with the effort per strata data
  group_by(month, anglerType) %>%
  mutate(
    strataDailyEffort = (nDays * meanDailyEffort) / ttlDays, # calculate mean daily effort per strata
    strataDailyEffort = ifelse(is.nan(strataDailyEffort), 0, strataDailyEffort), # if variance is not-a-number (i.e., 0/0) convert to a zero
    strataWeight = nDays / ttlDays, # create a weight of the number of days within a strata to the total number in the month
    var1 = (strataWeight^2 * varDailyEffort) / nDaysSampled, # variance calculations
    var2 = (strataWeight * varDailyEffort) / ttlDays
  ) %>%
  summarize(
    dailyEffortMean = sum(strataDailyEffort), # add the daily effort of the estimates
    dailyEffortVar = sum(var1) - sum(var2), # caulate variances
    seEffort = sqrt(dailyEffortVar), # calculate square roots
    ttlDays = mean(ttlDays)
  ) -> overallDailyEffort # carry forward the total days per month
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(overallDailyEffort, caption = "Monthly effort.", col.names = c("Month", "Angler type", "Mean daily effort", "Variance daily effort", "SE daily effort", "Total days"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```


*Extrapolate daily effort out to the entire month and calculate 95% confidence intervals around the estimate*

```{r}
overallDailyEffort %>%
  mutate(
    ttlEffort = dailyEffortMean * ttlDays, # monthly effort
    ttlVar = ttlDays^2 * dailyEffortVar, # monthly variance
    effortSE = seEffort * ttlDays, # monthly standard error
    effortCIhi = ttlEffort + 1.96 * seEffort, # confidence interval high
    effortCIlo = ttlEffort - 1.96 * seEffort
  ) %>% # confidence interval low
  select(-dailyEffortMean, -dailyEffortVar, -seEffort, -ttlDays) -> meanDailyEstimator # remove columns no longer needed
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(meanDailyEstimator, caption = "Monthly effort.", col.names = c("Month", "Angler type", "Total effort", "Variance Effort", "SE Effort", "95% CI high", "95% CI low"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```

*Strata estimator*

The strata estimator is similar to the mean daily estimator but instead of creating a mean day, it retains the mean and variance of the estimates within the strata.  To get monthly estimates, we extrapolate within strata and then add the estimates and variance.   A good example of the strata approach for for both effort and catch can be found in @rasmussen1998bias.  T

```{r rasmussen }
# Caculate daily estimates of effort the same as in the above example
juneCount %>%
  group_by(date, period, section) %>%
  summarise(
    meanBank = mean(bankAnglers),
    meanBoat = mean(boatAnglers),
    nCounts = n()
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    bankHours = meanBank * 8,
    boatHours = meanBoat * 8,
    bankDayHours = bankHours / 0.5,
    boatDayHours = boatHours / 0.5
  ) -> dailyEffort
```

*Calculate mean strata effort estimates*

```{r warning = FALSE}
dailyEffort %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(date, bankDayHours, boatDayHours) %>% # select specific comments
  pivot_longer(bankDayHours:boatDayHours, names_to = "anglerType", values_to = "dailyEffort") %>% # wide to long format
  mutate(
    anglerType = case_when(
      anglerType == "bankDayHours" ~ "Bank", # simplify angler type
      anglerType == "boatDayHours" ~ "Boat"
    ),
    date = ymd(date), # convert to date format
    month = month(date, label = TRUE), # add month
    strata = ifelse(wday(date, label = TRUE) %in% c("Sat", "Sun"), "weekendday", "weekday")
  ) %>% # add what kind of strata
  group_by(month, strata, anglerType) %>%
  summarise(
    meanStrataEffort = mean(dailyEffort), # mean effort
    sb2 = var(dailyEffort), # among day effort variance
    nSampled = n_distinct(date)
  ) %>% # number of days sampled
  left_join(daysPerMonth, by = c("month", "strata")) %>% # join to the number of days per month
  mutate(strataEffort = meanStrataEffort * nDays) -> effort2 # extrapolate effort within strata
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(effort2, caption = "Monthly effort by strata type.", col.names = c("Month", "Strata", "Angler type", "Mean effort", "Among day variance", "Days sampled", "Days in strata", "Total days", "Total effort"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```

*The strata estimator and multiple counts per shift allows us to include within daily variance to estimates*
In this process, we broke the components of variance into different components as described in @.
```{r}
juneCount %>%
  pivot_longer(bankAnglers:boatAnglers, names_to = "anglerType", values_to = "effort") %>%
  mutate(
    hours = effort * 8,
    dayHours = hours / 0.5
  ) %>% # extrapolate effort out
  group_by(date, anglerType) %>%
  mutate(
    meanEffort = mean(dayHours), # mean effort
    diffSq = (dayHours - meanEffort)^2
  ) %>% # difference from mean squared
  summarise(
    sumDiff = sum(diffSq), # sum of the squared difference
    nCounts = n()
  ) -> dailyVar1 # number of daily counts

# Within day variance
dailyVar1 %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    date = ymd(date), # date
    month = month(date, label = TRUE), # month
    strata = ifelse(wday(date, label = TRUE) %in% c("Sat", "Sun"), "weekendday", "weekday")
  ) %>% # strata
  # left_join(daysPerMonth, by = c("month", "strata")) %>%
  group_by(month, strata, anglerType, nCounts) %>%
  summarise(
    strataSum = sum(sumDiff), # sum of the difference
    nSampled = n()
  ) %>% # number of days sampled
  mutate(withinDayVar = strataSum / (nSampled * (nCounts - 1))) -> withinDayVar # caclulate within day variance

dailyVar1 %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    date = ymd(date), # date
    month = month(date, label = TRUE), # month
    strata = ifelse(wday(date, label = TRUE) %in% c("Sat", "Sun"), "weekendday", "weekday")
  ) %>% # strata
  group_by(month, strata, anglerType, nCounts) %>%
  summarise(
    strataSum = sum(sumDiff), # sum of the difference
    nSampled = n()
  ) %>% # number of days sampled
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    sw2 = strataSum / (nSampled * (nCounts - 1)), # component of variance
    anglerType = case_when(
      anglerType == "bankAnglers" ~ "Bank", # simplify angler type
      anglerType == "boatAnglers" ~ "Boat"
    )
  ) -> sw2

sw2 %>%
  select(month, strata, anglerType, sw2) %>%
  full_join(effort2) %>% # join with effort data
  mutate(
    var_y2bar = (((1 - (nSampled / nDays)) / nSampled) * sb2) + (1 / (2 * nDays)) * sw2, # component of variance
    strataVar = (nDays^2) * var_y2bar
  ) %>% # strata variance
  select(month, strata, anglerType, strataEffort, strataVar) %>%
  group_by(month, anglerType) %>%
  summarise(
    ttlEffort = sum(strataEffort), # monthly effort
    ttlVar = sum(strataVar)
  ) %>% # monthly variance
  mutate(
    effortSE = sqrt(ttlVar), # standard error
    effortCIlo = ttlEffort - 1.96 * effortSE, # confidence interval
    effortCIhi = ttlEffort + 1.96 * effortSE
  ) -> strataEstimators
strataEstimators
```


```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(strataEstimators, caption = "Monthly effort using a strata estimators.", col.names = c("Month", "Angler type", "Total effort", "Variance Effort", "SE Effort", "95% CI high", "95% CI low"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```


*Comparison of mean daily estimates and strata estimator method for assessing angler effort*
```{r comparisons, fig.cap = "Comparison of monthly efforts using a mean daily and a strata effort with different estimates of variation in a hypothetical bank and boat angler creel"}
rbind(
  data.frame(type = "Strata", strataEstimators), # combine strata and the mean daily estimator
  data.frame(type = "MeanDaily", meanDailyEstimator)
) -> effortEstimator


ggplot(data = effortEstimator, aes(x = anglerType, y = ttlEffort, ymin = effortCIlo, ymax = effortCIhi, color = type)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9), width = 0.5) +
  geom_point(aes(), size = 2, position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  labs(y = "Monthly Effort", x = "Angler type") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.5, 2.5), ylim = c(0, 30000), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

In this example, we can see that the point estimates are fairly consistent between the two methods.  The large difference is the amount of variance captured around the estimates.  There are a couple of reasons for this. The strata estimator included the within day variance and the mean daily did not.

\newpage

### Box 18.5.  Estimates of catch.

*Load libraries: tidyverse and lubridate.*
```{r loadpackages5, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(lubridate)
```

*Load interview data and strata effort data (from box 18.4), and check data.*

```{r loaddata5}
juneStrataEffort <- read_csv("data/juneStrataEffort.csv")
head(juneStrataEffort)

juneInterviews <- read_csv("data/juneInterviews.csv")
head(juneInterviews)
```


```{r echo = FALSE}
# kable(juneStrataEffort, caption = "The effort by angler type and strata from a creel survey.", col.names = c("Month","Strata", "Angler type","Strata effort", "Strata variance"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
# kable(head(juneInterviews), caption = "The first 6 rows of interviews from a creel survey.", col.names = c("Date","Period", "Section", "Number of anglers","Angler type", "Angler method","Time fished (hours)", "Time fished (mins)", "Trip type", "Num fish", "Catch type", "Species", "Species sought","Party ID", "Month", "Day of week", "Strata"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

When we calculate the catch rates, we must take into account whether the interview was complete or incomplete.  @pollock1997catch identified important issues concerning the point estimates and variances when catches were estimated from roving (mostly incomplete surveys) or from access point (complete surveys).  We will walk through an example of a hybrid approach that uses complete survey information and incomplete survey information.

*Look at the data to identify the number of complete and incomplete interviews*
```{r completedAccess, fig.cap = "The number of complete and incomplete intervals from a hypothetical creel survey"}
ggplot(juneInterviews) +
  geom_histogram(aes(x = tripType, fill = tripType), stat = "count", color = "black") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.5, 2.5), ylim = c(0, 200), expand = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Trip type", y = "Number") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

*Keep individual party information separate from the catch information*
```{r}
juneInterviews %>%
  distinct(partyID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(-numFish, -speciesCaught, -catchType) -> partyInfo
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
# kable(head(partyInfo), caption = "PArty information", col.names = c("Date","Period", "Section", "Number of anglers","Angler type", "Angler method","Time fished (hours)", "Time fished (mins)", "Trip type", "Species sought","Party ID", "Month", "Day of week", "Strata"), booktabs = TRUE)
```

*Calculate the number caught per party for all species*
```{r}
juneInterviews %>%
  select(partyID, speciesCaught, numFish, catchType) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = catchType, values_from = numFish, values_fill = list(numFish = 0)) %>% # move from long format to long format, easy way to fill in missing species
  mutate(C = H + R) %>% # calculate catch at the sum of fish harvested and fish released
  select(-`NA`) %>% # remove NAs
  pivot_longer(H:C, names_to = "catchType", values_to = "numCaught") %>% # move from wide format to long format
  filter(!is.na(speciesCaught)) %>% # remove missing species
  # group_by(partyID,  speciesCaught, catchType) %>%
  inner_join(partyInfo, by = "partyID") -> partyCatch # join to party information

head(partyCatch)
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
# kable(head(partyCatch), caption = "Party catch information", col.names = c("Prty ID", "Species caught", "Catch type", "Number caught", "Date","Period", "Section", "Number of anglers","Angler type", "Angler method","Time fished (hours)", "Time fished (mins)", "Trip type", "Species sought", "Month", "Day of week", "Strata"), booktabs = TRUE)
```


*Completed or access interviews*

```{r}
partyCatch %>%
  filter(tripType == "complete") %>%
  mutate(partyEffort = ((timeFishedHours * 60 + timeFishedMins) / 60) * numAnglers) %>% # effort in decimal hours
  group_by(month, strata, anglerType, speciesCaught, catchType) %>%
  summarise(
    ttlNum = sum(numCaught), # total number caught
    ttlAnglers = sum(numAnglers), # total number of anglers
    ttlTime = sum(partyEffort), # tota effort
    varF = var(numCaught), # variance of fish caught
    varH = var(partyEffort), # variance of party effort
    fbar2 = mean(numCaught)^2,
    hbar2 = mean(partyEffort)^2,
    fhbar2 = mean(numCaught * partyEffort)^2,
    covfh = cov(numCaught, partyEffort), # covariance
    k = n_distinct(partyID)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    rate = (ttlNum / ttlAnglers) / (ttlTime), # ratio of the means
    varRate = ((1 / k) * rate^2) * ((varF / fbar2) + ((varH / hbar2)) + (covfh / fhbar2)), # variance of the catch rates
    varRate = ifelse(is.nan(varRate), 0, varRate)
  ) %>% # correct for is not a number
  select(-ttlAnglers, -ttlTime, -ttlNum, -varF, -varH, -fbar2, -hbar2, -fhbar2, -covfh, k) -> strataComplRates
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(strataComplRates, caption = "Party catch rates from complete trips", col.names = c("Month", "Strata", "Angler type", "Species caught", "Catch type", "Number", "Rate", "Variance rate"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 3)
```


*Extrapolate the catch rates to the month*

```{r}
left_join(strataComplRates, juneStrataEffort, by = c("month", "strata", "anglerType")) %>%
  mutate(
    strataFish = rate * strataEffort,
    strataFishVar = (strataEffort^2 * varRate) + (rate^2 * strataVar) - (varRate * strataVar)
  ) %>%
  select(month:catchType, strataFish, strataFishVar) -> completeFish
```


```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(completeFish %>% as.data.frame(), caption = "Monthly catches", col.names = c("Month", "Strata", "Angler type", "Species caught", "Catch type", "Total number", "Number variance"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```

*Roving or incomplete surveys*
```{r rovingRoving}
# Mean of the ratios
partyCatch %>%
  filter(
    tripType == "incomplete", # filter to incomplete surveys
    (timeFishedHours * 60 + timeFishedMins) >= 30
  ) %>% # truncate surveys to those more than 30 minutes
  group_by(month, partyID, strata, anglerType, speciesCaught, catchType) %>%
  mutate(
    partyEffort = ((timeFishedHours * 60 + timeFishedMins) / 60) * numAnglers, # party effort
    rate = numCaught / partyEffort
  ) %>% # party catch rates
  group_by(month, strata, anglerType, speciesCaught, catchType) %>%
  summarise(
    sumRate2 = sum(rate^2), # sum of rate squared
    sumRate = sum(rate), # sum of rates
    k = n_distinct(partyID), # number of parties
    meanRate = mean(rate)
  ) %>% # mean rate
  mutate(
    varRate = (sumRate2 - ((sumRate^2) / k)) / (k * (k - 1)), # variance of rates
    varRate = ifelse(is.nan(varRate), 0, varRate)
  ) %>% # correct rates for not-a-numbers
  select(month, strata, anglerType, speciesCaught, catchType, k, meanRate, varRate) -> strataIncomplRates
```

```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(strataIncomplRates, caption = "Party catch rates from incomplete trips", col.names = c("Month", "Strata", "Angler type", "Species caught", "Catch type", "Number", "Rate", "Variance rate"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 3)
```

*Extrapolate the catch rates to the month*
```{r rovingRoving2}
left_join(strataIncomplRates, juneStrataEffort) %>%
  mutate(
    strataFish = meanRate * strataEffort,
    strataFishVar = (strataEffort^2 * varRate) + (meanRate^2 * strataVar) - (varRate * strataVar)
  ) %>%
  select(month:catchType, strataFish, strataFishVar) -> incompleteFish
```



```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(incompleteFish %>% as.data.frame(), caption = "Monthly catches from incompleted trips", col.names = c("Month", "Strata", "Angler type", "Species caught", "Catch type", "Total number", "Number variance"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```

*Combine the incomplete and complete surveys*
```{r combinetypes}
rbind(incompleteFish, completeFish) %>%
  group_by(month, strata, anglerType, speciesCaught, catchType) %>%
  summarise(
    numFish = sum(strataFish), # add across strata
    fishVar = sum(strataFish)
  ) %>%
  group_by(month, anglerType, speciesCaught, catchType) %>%
  summarise(
    monthFish = sum(numFish), # total number
    monthVar = sum(fishVar)
  ) %>% # total variance
  mutate(
    monthSE = sqrt(monthVar), # standard error
    CIlo = monthFish - monthSE * 1.96, # 95% confidence interval
    CIhi = monthFish + monthSE * 1.96
  ) -> fishTotal
```

*Create a graph of the number of species caught*
```{r combinetypesgraph, fig.cap = "The number and 95% confidence interval of several species caught by boat and bank anglers in a hypothetical creel "}
ggplot(fishTotal %>% filter(catchType == "C"), aes(x = speciesCaught, y = monthFish, ymin = CIlo, ymax = CIhi, fill = anglerType)) +
  geom_col(color = "black", position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  geom_errorbar(position = position_dodge(0.9)) +
  labs(x = "Species", y = "Number", fill = "Angler type") +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0.5, 5.5), ylim = c(0, 3000), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = c(0.75, 0.75))
```


### Box 18.6.  Using bipartite networks to explore the relationship beteween angler seeking behavior and released fish.

*Load libraries: tidyverse and bipartite*
```{r loadpackages6, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(bipartite)
```

*Load interview data and look at data.*
```{r loaddata6, message=FALSE}
juneInterviews <- read_csv("data/juneInterviews.csv")
```

*Keep individual party information separate from the catch information*
```{r}
juneInterviews %>%
  distinct(partyID, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  select(-numFish, -speciesCaught, -catchType) -> partyInfo
```

*Calculate the number caught per party for all species*
```{r partycatch}
juneInterviews %>%
  select(partyID, speciesCaught, numFish, catchType) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = catchType, values_from = numFish, values_fill = list(numFish = 0)) %>% # move from long format to long format, easy way to fill in missing species
  mutate(C = H + R) %>% # calculate catch at the sum of fish harvested and fish released
  select(-`NA`) %>% # remove NAs
  pivot_longer(H:C, names_to = "catchType", values_to = "numCaught") %>% # move from wide format to long format
  filter(
    !is.na(speciesCaught),
    catchType == "R"
  ) %>% # keep only released information
  inner_join(partyInfo, by = "partyID") %>% # join with party information
  mutate(
    partyEffort = ((timeFishedHours * 60 + timeFishedMins) / 60) * numAnglers, # party effort
    CPUE = numCaught / partyEffort
  ) -> partyCatch
```

*Calculate the number of each species released (standardized to 3 hours of angling).*
```{r seekingcatch}
partyCatch %>%
  group_by(anglerMethod, speciesSought, speciesCaught) %>%
  summarise(
    mCPUE = mean(CPUE), # mean catch per unit effort
    Catch3hr = ceiling(mCPUE * 3)
  ) %>% # number caught during three hours, rounded up to the nearest whole number
  ungroup() %>%
  select(-anglerMethod, -mCPUE) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = speciesSought, values_from = Catch3hr, values_fill = list(Catch3hr = 0)) %>% # long to wide format
  as.data.frame() -> seekCaught # convert to data.frame
```

*Convert to a matrix*
```{r seekingcatch2}
rownames(seekCaught) <- seekCaught$speciesCaught # species released as a row name
seekCaught <- seekCaught[, -1] # remove species released column
seekCaught <- as.matrix(seekCaught) # convert to a matrix
```


```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(seekCaught, caption = "Species seeking and release matrix", col.names = c("Anything", "Channel Catfish", "Walleye", "White Bass"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```


*View the bipartite network*
```{r seekingcatch3, fig.cap = "A bipartite network representing species seeking groups (top) and the species released (bottom).  The width of the bars represent the proportional number seeking and released."}
plotweb(seekCaught,
  labsize = 1, # text.rot=90,
  col.high = "#d8b365",
  bor.col.high = "white",
  col.low = "#5ab4ac",
  bor.col.low = "white",
  col.interaction = "#f5f5f5",
  text.high.col = "black",
  text.low.col = "black",
  bor.col.interaction = "#999999"
)
```

*Create a function to extract several network metrics*
```{r}
get_the_info <- function(df) {
  networklvl <- networklevel(df)
  grouplvl <- grouplevel(df)

  outDat <- data.frame(
    HL.num = grouplvl["number.of.species.HL"],
    LL.num = grouplvl["number.of.species.LL"],
    connectance = networklvl["connectance"],
    shannon = networklvl["Shannon diversity"],
    cluster = networklvl["cluster coefficient"],
    w.nested = networklvl["weighted nestedness"],
    h2 = networklvl["H2"],
    HL.links = grouplvl["mean.number.of.links.HL"],
    LL.links = grouplvl["mean.number.of.links.LL"],
    HL.C_score = grouplvl["C.score.HL"],
    LL.C_score = grouplvl["C.score.LL"],
    HL.togetherness = grouplvl["togetherness.HL"],
    LL.togetherness = grouplvl["togetherness.LL"]
  )

  return(outDat)
}

get_the_info(seekCaught) %>%
  pivot_longer(HL.num:LL.togetherness, names_to = "metric", values_to = "value") -> networkVals
```


```{r echo = FALSE}
kable(networkVals, caption = "Bipartite network metrics", col.names = c("Metric", "Value"), booktabs = TRUE, digits = 1)
```

#### References
